code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Complete functional failure despite proper usage attempts
    - Multi-purchase defects with safety hazards
    - Core usability failures preventing basic functionality
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence
    - Negative updates erasing previous satisfaction

    2★ (Negative):
    - Persistent frustration despite occasional success
    - Requires excessive workarounds for core functionality
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - Partial functionality with recurring issues

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Conditional recommendations without endorsement
    - Target-specific adequacy with reservations

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Requires break-in period for optimal performance
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - "Only reason not 5" explicit deductions

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Effective need fulfillment without functional flaws
    - 3+ complementary aspects praised (function/design/durability)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Concise praise indicating precise need fulfillment
    - Close brand comparisons with satisfaction + minor differences
    - Practical benefits significantly outweigh stated limitations

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works exactly as needed" →5 even if concise
    - Functional praise + usage limitation →4
    - Praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Brief effectiveness praise →5 unless criticism exists
    - Core function achieved post-setup →5 if flawless
    - "Still 5 stars" endorsements override minor flaws
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Wax and wicks. They work just fine."
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Sharp tablecloth. Only con is needing to iron cotton"
<Reasoning>Non-functional criticism with core praise →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Does the job but constantly needs readjusting"
<Reasoning>Excessive maintenance for basic function →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Good for light use. Thin material concerns"
<Reasoning>Adequate with clear limitations →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Rollers handle bumps perfectly! Tough installation"
<Reasoning>Core excellence outweighs setup effort →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Innovative concept fails during actual use"
<Reasoning>Core usability failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Works great after 2 washes. Stiff edges remain"
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "SolvES our organization needs perfectly. Plain design."
<Reasoning>Functional excellence overrides aesthetics →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (27 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 4
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 3: 1
  gold 2 -> 1: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [5, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 3, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "When it comes to cookware cast iron is about as durable as it gets. Yes, it can be a bit harder to take care of (don't throw it in the dishwasher and towel dry it after washing) but it will literally last generations. This skillet came pre-seasoned w..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 27 errors, 22 borderline out of 80 examples."}
